findings.title: SQL injection practice surface (Acunetix test site)
findings.description: Public demo site is designed with SQL injection vectors in login and search parameters.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries; never concatenate user input into SQL.

findings.title: Reflected/stored XSS practice surface
findings.description: Demo endpoints accept unsanitized input suitable for XSS testing in authorized labs only.
findings.remediation: Encode output contextually; deploy CSP; sanitize input.

findings.title: Weak session handling (demo)
findings.description: Test applications often ship predictable session identifiers for training.
findings.remediation: Rotate session IDs on login; mark cookies Secure and HttpOnly.

findings.title: OWASP Juice Shop — broken access control challenges
findings.description: Intentionally vulnerable SPA includes IDOR and privilege escalation training scenarios.
findings.remediation: Enforce server-side authorization on every object reference.

findings.title: OWASP Juice Shop — NoSQL / injection challenges
findings.description: Juice Shop includes API injection flaws for security education.
findings.remediation: Validate and parameterize all API inputs; use ODM/query builders safely.

findings.title: OWASP Juice Shop — sensitive data exposure
findings.description: Training app exposes secrets and weak crypto patterns by design.
findings.remediation: Encrypt sensitive data at rest; never embed secrets in client bundles.

findings.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (demo banking app)
findings.description: Practice banking application includes predictable account identifiers for IDOR labs.
findings.remediation: Bind object access to authenticated user context server-side.

findings.title: Verbose error messages (demo)
findings.description: Training apps may leak stack traces to aid learning.
findings.remediation: Return generic errors to clients; log details server-side only.
